validation_details.title: Workforce Planning Analyst
validation_details.reason: Specific and functional; clearly suggests workforce planning work.

validation_details.title: Workforce Planning Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific and functional; understandable to outside jobseekers.

validation_details.title: Policy Analyst
validation_details.reason: Too broad and generic. It does not indicate what policy area or type of analysis.

validation_details.title: Budget Analyst
validation_details.reason: Common private-sector title and sufficiently descriptive.

validation_details.title: Program Evaluation Analyst
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to convey evaluation-focused analytical work.

validation_details.title: Administrative Coordinator
validation_details.reason: Still too generic. It describes an administrative role but not the functional area or type of coordination.

validation_details.title: Administrative Operations Manager
validation_details.reason: Too broad and federal-sounding. 'Administrative operations' does not clearly tell a jobseeker what domain or work is managed.

validation_details.title: Continuous Improvement Manager
validation_details.reason: Recognizable private-sector title and reasonably descriptive.

validation_details.title: Operations Planning Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate planning within operations.

validation_details.title: Chief of Staff
validation_details.reason: Widely recognized title with clear meaning in many organizations.

validation_details.title: Clinical Operations Analyst
validation_details.reason: Clear enough to indicate analysis of clinical operations.

validation_details.title: Contract Administrator
validation_details.reason: Common private-sector title and sufficiently descriptive.

validation_details.title: Financial Planning Analyst
validation_details.reason: Clear and common in the private sector.

validation_details.title: Academic Operations Coordinator
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate coordination in an academic operations setting.

validation_details.title: Administrative Services Supervisor
validation_details.reason: A bit broad, but still functional and understandable as a supervisory administrative role.

validation_details.title: Business Analyst
validation_details.reason: Common private-sector title; while broad, it is still a recognized functional title.

validation_details.title: Data Governance Manager
validation_details.reason: Recognizable private-sector title and specific enough.

validation_details.title: Education Program Analyst
validation_details.reason: Clear enough to indicate analysis of education programs.

validation_details.title: IT Acquisition Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific enough, though 'IT' is an abbreviation; it is widely understood but slightly less ideal than fully spelled-out language.

validation_details.title: Management Consultant
validation_details.reason: Common private-sector title and sufficiently descriptive.

validation_details.title: Manpower Planning Manager
validation_details.reason: 'Manpower' is dated and federal jargon; it is less plain-language and less inclusive than modern workforce terminology.

validation_details.title: Personnel Actions Specialist
validation_details.reason: Sounds like federal HR jargon and does not clearly convey the broader work area to outside jobseekers.

validation_details.title: Requirements Analyst
validation_details.reason: Recognizable private-sector title, though somewhat broad.

validation_details.title: Resource Planning Analyst
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate planning and analysis work.

validation_details.title: Application Processing Analyst
validation_details.reason: Clear enough to indicate work involving application processing.

validation_details.title: Business Systems Analyst
validation_details.reason: Common private-sector title and sufficiently descriptive.

validation_details.title: Configuration Control Manager
validation_details.reason: Recognizable technical title and sufficiently descriptive.

validation_details.title: Contracting Officer Representative
validation_details.reason: This is a federal designation/acronym-style role title rather than plain-language private-sector terminology.

validation_details.title: Defense Acquisition Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate acquisition work in the defense domain.

validation_details.title: Health IT Workflow Analyst
validation_details.reason: Specific and understandable, though 'IT' is an abbreviation.

validation_details.title: IT Procurement Specialist
validation_details.reason: Specific and understandable, though 'IT' is an abbreviation.

validation_details.title: IT Project Coordinator
validation_details.reason: Specific and understandable, though 'IT' is an abbreviation.

validation_details.title: IT Systems Analyst
validation_details.reason: Common and understandable, though 'IT' is an abbreviation.

validation_details.title: Legislative Analyst
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate analysis of legislation.

validation_details.title: Logistics Program Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate program management in logistics.

validation_details.title: Operations Coordinator
validation_details.reason: Common and understandable title, though broad.

validation_details.title: Operations Support Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate management of support operations.

validation_details.title: Project Controls Manager
validation_details.reason: Recognizable private-sector title and sufficiently descriptive.

validation_details.title: Project Coordinator
validation_details.reason: Common and understandable title, though broad.

validation_details.title: Security Program Manager
validation_details.reason: Specific enough to indicate management of a security program.

validation_details.title: Strategic Communications Director
validation_details.reason: Common private-sector title and sufficiently descriptive.

titles.title: Workforce Planning Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes workforce data, staffing trends, and organizational needs to help leaders forecast hiring, retention, and skill gaps. Builds reports and recommendations for workforce plans and staffing strategies.

titles.title: Workforce Planning Manager
titles.description: Leads workforce planning efforts across an organization, overseeing staffing forecasts, position management, and long-range labor planning. Coordinates with HR and leadership to align workforce strategy with mission needs.

titles.title: Defense Policy Analyst
titles.description: Researches and analyzes defense-related policies, programs, and issues to support decision-making and policy development. Prepares briefs, recommendations, and background materials for leaders and stakeholders.

titles.title: HR Policy Analyst
titles.description: Reviews human resources policies, procedures, and regulations to ensure they are current and compliant. Drafts policy updates, analyzes impacts, and advises on HR policy implementation.

titles.title: Regulatory Affairs Analyst
titles.description: Interprets regulations and helps ensure programs, products, or operations comply with applicable rules. Prepares compliance documentation, tracks regulatory changes, and supports responses to regulators.

titles.title: Legislative Analyst
titles.description: Tracks legislation, congressional activity, and policy proposals that affect the organization. Summarizes bills, prepares issue papers, and advises leaders on legislative impacts.

titles.title: Budget Analyst
titles.description: Develops, monitors, and analyzes budgets for programs or offices. Reviews spending, prepares budget justifications, and helps managers make funding decisions.

titles.title: Defense Budget Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes defense program budgets, funding requests, and execution data to support military or defense agency planning. Prepares budget exhibits, forecasts, and justification materials.

titles.title: Healthcare Data Analyst
titles.description: Collects, cleans, and analyzes healthcare data to identify trends in patient care, operations, or outcomes. Produces reports and dashboards that support clinical and administrative decisions.

titles.title: Program Evaluation Analyst
titles.description: Evaluates whether programs are meeting goals, using performance data, metrics, and qualitative information. Recommends improvements based on findings and prepares evaluation reports.

titles.title: Operations Coordinator
titles.description: Coordinates day-to-day operational tasks, schedules, and follow-up actions to keep office or program activities running smoothly. Tracks requests, communicates with stakeholders, and helps resolve routine issues.

titles.title: Project Coordinator
titles.description: Supports project managers by tracking tasks, schedules, deliverables, and meeting actions. Helps organize project documentation, communications, and status updates.

titles.title: Executive Assistant
titles.description: Provides high-level administrative support to executives, managing calendars, correspondence, travel, and meeting logistics. Often prepares briefing materials and handles confidential information.

titles.title: Administrative Services Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises administrative support staff and oversees office services such as records, scheduling, supplies, and correspondence. Ensures administrative processes run efficiently and meet organizational needs.

titles.title: Operations Support Manager
titles.description: Manages support functions that keep operations running, such as coordination, reporting, logistics, and administrative services. Improves workflows and ensures teams have the resources they need.

titles.title: Administrative Services Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises administrative support staff and oversees office services such as records, scheduling, supplies, and correspondence. Ensures administrative processes run efficiently and meet organizational needs.

titles.title: Office Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees daily office operations, including administrative processes, staffing, facilities coordination, and workflow management. Ensures the office functions efficiently and supports leadership needs.

titles.title: Business Operations Manager
titles.description: Manages business processes and operational support activities to improve efficiency, service delivery, and coordination across teams. Tracks performance, resolves operational issues, and implements process improvements.

titles.title: Continuous Improvement Manager
titles.description: Leads process improvement efforts using data, workflow analysis, and performance metrics to reduce waste and improve efficiency. Guides teams in adopting better methods and standardizing practices.

titles.title: Operations Planning Manager
titles.description: Plans and coordinates operational activities, staffing, and resources to meet short- and long-term mission requirements. Develops schedules, forecasts needs, and aligns operations with organizational goals.

titles.title: Program Budget Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes and tracks budgets for specific programs, ensuring funds are allocated and spent appropriately. Prepares budget plans, forecasts, and financial reports for program managers.

titles.title: Quality Assurance Specialist
titles.description: Reviews processes, outputs, or services to ensure they meet established standards and requirements. Conducts audits, identifies defects or gaps, and recommends corrective actions.

titles.title: Chief of Staff
titles.description: Serves as a senior coordinator for an executive or leadership office, managing priorities, communications, and follow-through on key initiatives. Often oversees staff, prepares decision materials, and ensures leadership actions are executed.

titles.title: Clinical Informatics Specialist
titles.description: Supports the use of health information systems and clinical data to improve patient care and workflow. Works with clinicians and IT staff to configure systems, analyze data, and troubleshoot issues.

titles.title: Clinical Operations Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes clinical workflows, staffing, and operational data to improve patient care delivery and efficiency. Produces reports and recommendations for clinic or hospital leadership.

titles.title: Contract Administrator
titles.description: Manages contract paperwork, compliance, modifications, and correspondence throughout the contract lifecycle. Tracks deliverables, deadlines, and contractor performance to ensure terms are met.

titles.title: Executive Assistant
titles.description: Provides high-level administrative support to executives, managing calendars, correspondence, travel, and meeting logistics. Often prepares briefing materials and handles confidential information.

titles.title: Financial Planning Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes financial data, forecasts, and spending trends to support planning and decision-making. Builds models, prepares reports, and helps managers plan future resource needs.

titles.title: Human Resources Advisor
titles.description: Advises managers and employees on HR matters such as staffing, employee relations, policy interpretation, and workforce issues. Helps resolve personnel questions and ensures actions follow HR rules.

titles.title: Maintenance Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes maintenance records, equipment performance data, and repair trends to improve reliability and scheduling. Produces reports that help maintenance teams plan work and reduce downtime.

titles.title: Project Budget Analyst
titles.description: Tracks project funding, expenditures, and forecasts to ensure projects stay within budget. Prepares financial reports, funding requests, and cost analyses for project leaders.

titles.title: Academic Operations Coordinator
titles.description: Coordinates administrative and operational tasks that support academic programs, schedules, and student or faculty services. Handles communications, records, and routine process follow-up.

titles.title: Administrative Services Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises administrative support staff and oversees office services such as records, scheduling, supplies, and correspondence. Ensures administrative processes run efficiently and meet organizational needs.

titles.title: Aircraft Maintenance Data Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes aircraft maintenance data, repair histories, and readiness metrics to support fleet maintenance decisions. Identifies trends, tracks performance, and helps improve maintenance planning.

titles.title: Business Analyst
titles.description: Studies business processes, requirements, and data to identify problems and recommend improvements. Works with stakeholders to document needs, analyze options, and support implementation.

titles.title: Construction Budget Analyst
titles.description: Monitors construction project budgets, costs, and forecasts to keep projects financially on track. Prepares funding reports, cost analyses, and budget justifications.

titles.title: Contract Compliance Specialist
titles.description: Reviews contracts and contractor performance to verify compliance with terms and requirements. Tracks deliverables, documents issues, and supports enforcement or remediation.

titles.title: Data Governance Manager
titles.description: Leads policies and processes for managing data quality, ownership, access, and standards across an organization. Coordinates with stakeholders to ensure data is accurate, secure, and usable.

titles.title: Defense Policy Advisor
titles.description: Advises leaders on defense policy issues, strategic options, and implications of proposed actions. Prepares analysis, talking points, and recommendations for decision-makers.

titles.title: Education Program Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes education programs, performance data, and service delivery to assess effectiveness and identify improvements. Supports planning, reporting, and program evaluation activities.

titles.title: Executive Communications Specialist
titles.description: Develops speeches, talking points, memos, and other communications for senior leaders. Shapes messaging, prepares briefing materials, and helps manage executive-level communications.

titles.title: HR Policy Analyst
titles.description: Reviews human resources policies, procedures, and regulations to ensure they are current and compliant. Drafts policy updates, analyzes impacts, and advises on HR policy implementation.

titles.title: Health Informatics Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes health data and information systems to improve clinical workflows, reporting, and decision support. Works with healthcare staff and IT teams to translate data needs into system solutions.

titles.title: Human Resources Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes HR data, staffing actions, and personnel processes to support workforce management. Prepares reports, tracks trends, and helps resolve HR process issues.

titles.title: IT Acquisition Manager
titles.description: Manages acquisition activities for information technology products and services, including requirements, sourcing, and vendor coordination. Ensures purchases align with technical needs, budgets, and procurement rules.

titles.title: Internal Controls Manager
titles.description: Oversees internal control processes to reduce risk, ensure compliance, and improve accountability. Reviews procedures, tests controls, and coordinates corrective actions for weaknesses.

titles.title: Maintenance Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes maintenance systems, data, and workflows to improve equipment tracking, scheduling, and reliability. Supports system configuration, reporting, and process improvements for maintenance operations.

titles.title: Management Consultant
titles.description: Advises organizations on improving strategy, operations, structure, and performance. Conducts analysis, interviews stakeholders, and develops recommendations for change.

titles.title: Workforce Planning Manager
titles.description: Leads workforce planning efforts across an organization, overseeing staffing forecasts, position management, and long-range labor planning. Coordinates with HR and leadership to align workforce strategy with mission needs.

titles.title: Workforce Planning Specialist
titles.description: Supports workforce planning by analyzing staffing data, position needs, and workforce trends. Helps develop forecasts, reports, and recommendations for hiring and resource planning.

titles.title: Civilian Workforce Planner
titles.description: Plans civilian staffing needs by analyzing vacancies, workforce trends, and organizational requirements. Helps leaders forecast hiring, manage positions, and align staffing with mission priorities.

titles.title: Workforce Programs Manager
titles.description: Manages workforce-related programs such as staffing, development, retention, or succession planning. Oversees program performance, policy implementation, and coordination with HR stakeholders.

titles.title: HR Operations Specialist
titles.description: Handles day-to-day human resources transactions and operational processes such as onboarding, personnel actions, records, and benefits support. Ensures HR work is accurate, timely, and compliant.

titles.title: Human Resources Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes HR data, staffing actions, and personnel processes to support workforce management. Prepares reports, tracks trends, and helps resolve HR process issues.

titles.title: Employee Relations Specialist
titles.description: Advises managers and employees on workplace conduct, grievances, disciplinary actions, and conflict resolution. Investigates issues, interprets policy, and helps resolve employee relations matters.

titles.title: Recruitment Specialist
titles.description: Plans and executes recruiting activities to attract and hire qualified candidates. Posts jobs, screens applicants, coordinates interviews, and supports hiring managers.

titles.title: Procurement Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes purchasing data, vendor performance, and procurement processes to improve buying decisions and compliance. Prepares reports, tracks spending, and supports sourcing activities.

titles.title: Procurement Specialist
titles.description: Handles purchasing actions, vendor coordination, and procurement documentation for goods and services. Ensures acquisitions follow policy, budget, and competition requirements.

titles.title: Requirements Analyst
titles.description: Gathers and documents business or technical requirements for systems, services, or projects. Works with stakeholders to clarify needs and translate them into actionable specifications.

titles.title: Resource Planning Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes staffing, funding, equipment, or other resource needs to support planning and allocation decisions. Develops forecasts and reports to help managers balance demand and capacity.

titles.title: Technical Program Manager
titles.description: Coordinates technical projects or programs, aligning engineering, IT, or operations teams around schedules, deliverables, and risks. Tracks progress, resolves blockers, and ensures technical work meets program goals.

titles.title: Training Program Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes training needs, program performance, and learner outcomes to improve training offerings. Supports curriculum planning, reporting, and evaluation of training effectiveness.

titles.title: Training Program Manager
titles.description: Manages training programs, including planning, delivery, staffing, and performance tracking. Ensures training initiatives meet organizational needs and improve employee capability.

titles.title: Aircraft Maintenance Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes aircraft maintenance data, readiness, and repair trends to support fleet sustainment and scheduling. Helps identify maintenance issues and improve aircraft availability.

titles.title: Application Processing Analyst
titles.description: Reviews and processes applications, requests, or case files to ensure completeness and compliance with requirements. Tracks status, resolves discrepancies, and supports timely decisions.

titles.title: Budget and Planning Manager
titles.description: Leads budget development and planning activities, aligning resources with organizational priorities. Oversees forecasts, funding plans, and financial analysis for management decisions.

titles.title: Business Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes business processes and system requirements to improve how technology supports operations. Works with users and developers to define needs, test solutions, and support implementation.

titles.title: Child Care Program Manager
titles.description: Manages child care programs, including staffing, operations, compliance, and service quality. Oversees schedules, budgets, and program improvements to support families and children.

titles.title: Civilian Workforce Planner
titles.description: Plans civilian staffing needs by analyzing vacancies, workforce trends, and organizational requirements. Helps leaders forecast hiring, manage positions, and align staffing with mission priorities.

titles.title: Clinic Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees daily clinic operations, including staffing, patient flow, scheduling, and administrative processes. Works to improve efficiency, service quality, and coordination among clinical teams.

titles.title: Clinical Informatics Manager
titles.description: Leads teams that use health information systems and clinical data to improve care delivery and operations. Oversees system optimization, reporting, and coordination between clinicians and IT.

titles.title: Clinical Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes and supports clinical software and workflows used in healthcare settings. Helps configure systems, troubleshoot issues, and improve how clinical staff use technology.

titles.title: Configuration Control Manager
titles.description: Manages configuration management processes to track and control changes to systems, equipment, or documentation. Ensures versions, approvals, and baselines are maintained accurately.

titles.title: Congressional Affairs Specialist
titles.description: Manages communications and coordination with Congress, including inquiries, briefings, and legislative responses. Prepares materials and tracks issues affecting the organization.

titles.title: Contract Compliance Manager
titles.description: Oversees contract compliance activities, ensuring contractors meet terms, reporting requirements, and regulatory obligations. Reviews performance issues and coordinates corrective actions.

titles.title: Contract Compliance Specialist
titles.description: Reviews contracts and contractor performance to verify compliance with terms and requirements. Tracks deliverables, documents issues, and supports enforcement or remediation.

titles.title: Contract Administrator
titles.description: Manages contract paperwork, compliance, modifications, and correspondence throughout the contract lifecycle. Tracks deliverables, deadlines, and contractor performance to ensure terms are met.

titles.title: Vendor Manager
titles.description: Manages relationships with vendors and monitors service levels, deliverables, and contract performance. Coordinates issue resolution and helps ensure vendors meet business needs.

titles.title: Procurement Specialist
titles.description: Handles purchasing actions, vendor coordination, and procurement documentation for goods and services. Ensures acquisitions follow policy, budget, and competition requirements.

titles.title: Contracts and Procurement Manager
titles.description: Leads contract and procurement activities, overseeing sourcing, negotiations, compliance, and vendor performance. Ensures purchases and contracts support organizational goals and follow regulations.

titles.title: Data Analytics Manager
titles.description: Leads analysts in collecting, interpreting, and presenting data to support business or mission decisions. Oversees dashboards, reporting, and analytical methods to improve performance insights.

titles.title: Defense Acquisition Manager
titles.description: Manages acquisition programs for defense goods or services, coordinating requirements, procurement, and contract execution. Tracks milestones, budgets, and compliance across the acquisition lifecycle.

titles.title: Defense Policy Analyst
titles.description: Researches and analyzes defense-related policies, programs, and issues to support decision-making and policy development. Prepares briefs, recommendations, and background materials for leaders and stakeholders.

titles.title: Depot Maintenance Manager
titles.description: Oversees depot-level maintenance operations, including scheduling, staffing, production, and quality control. Ensures equipment or vehicles are repaired efficiently and meet readiness requirements.

titles.title: Employee Relations Specialist
titles.description: Advises managers and employees on workplace conduct, grievances, disciplinary actions, and conflict resolution. Investigates issues, interprets policy, and helps resolve employee relations matters.

titles.title: Enterprise Data Manager
titles.description: Manages enterprise-wide data assets, standards, and governance practices to improve data quality and accessibility. Coordinates data stewardship, reporting needs, and cross-functional data initiatives.

titles.title: Environmental Budget Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes budgets and spending for environmental programs, projects, or compliance activities. Prepares forecasts, funding justifications, and financial reports for program leaders.

titles.title: Facilities Planning Specialist
titles.description: Plans space, facility, and infrastructure needs based on organizational growth, occupancy, and mission requirements. Develops recommendations for renovations, relocations, and long-term facilities use.

titles.title: HR Operations Specialist
titles.description: Handles day-to-day human resources transactions and operational processes such as onboarding, personnel actions, records, and benefits support. Ensures HR work is accurate, timely, and compliant.

titles.title: HR Operations Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises HR operations staff and oversees personnel transactions, records management, onboarding, and related administrative processes. Ensures HR services are accurate, timely, and compliant.

titles.title: Health IT Workflow Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes healthcare workflows and information technology processes to improve how clinical staff use health systems. Identifies bottlenecks, documents requirements, and supports workflow redesign.

titles.title: Healthcare Operations Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes healthcare operations data, workflows, and performance metrics to improve service delivery and efficiency. Prepares reports and recommendations for clinical or administrative leaders.

titles.title: Human Resources Manager
titles.description: Leads HR functions such as staffing, employee relations, policy, performance management, and workforce planning. Oversees HR staff and ensures personnel practices support organizational goals.

titles.title: IT Procurement Specialist
titles.description: Supports purchasing of information technology products and services by developing requirements, coordinating vendors, and processing procurement actions. Ensures acquisitions meet technical and policy requirements.

titles.title: IT Project Coordinator
titles.description: Supports IT projects by tracking tasks, schedules, documentation, and communications among technical teams and stakeholders. Helps keep project activities organized and on schedule.

titles.title: IT Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes information technology systems, user needs, and technical issues to improve system performance and support business operations. Works with users and developers to define requirements and troubleshoot problems.

titles.title: Immigration Process Analyst
titles.description: Reviews immigration-related cases, workflows, and documentation to improve processing accuracy and timeliness. Tracks requirements, identifies bottlenecks, and supports compliance with immigration rules.

titles.title: Internal Auditor
titles.description: Examines processes, controls, and records to assess compliance, accuracy, and risk. Conducts audits, documents findings, and recommends corrective actions to improve accountability.

titles.title: Legislative Affairs Specialist
titles.description: Coordinates legislative tracking, congressional communications, and responses to policy or oversight issues. Prepares briefings, correspondence, and analysis for leadership.

titles.title: Legislative Analyst
titles.description: Tracks legislation, congressional activity, and policy proposals that affect the organization. Summarizes bills, prepares issue papers, and advises leaders on legislative impacts.

titles.title: Logistics Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes supply, transportation, inventory, and distribution data to improve logistics performance. Identifies bottlenecks and recommends changes to support efficient movement of goods and materials.

titles.title: Logistics Compliance Manager
titles.description: Oversees compliance with logistics policies, regulations, and contractual requirements. Monitors processes, resolves issues, and ensures supply chain activities meet standards.

titles.title: Logistics Program Manager
titles.description: Manages logistics programs and coordinates supply, transportation, inventory, and distribution activities. Oversees schedules, resources, and performance to support mission needs.

titles.title: Maintenance Operations Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes maintenance operations data, work orders, and performance metrics to improve efficiency and readiness. Supports planning, reporting, and process improvement for maintenance teams.

titles.title: Maintenance Operations Manager
titles.description: Manages maintenance operations, including staffing, scheduling, workflow, and performance monitoring. Ensures maintenance work is completed safely, efficiently, and on time.

titles.title: Maintenance Planning Analyst
titles.description: Plans maintenance activities by analyzing equipment status, schedules, and resource needs. Helps coordinate preventive maintenance and prioritize work to reduce downtime.

titles.title: Maintenance Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers maintenance information systems, including user support, data integrity, and system configuration. Ensures maintenance records and workflows are accurately maintained in the system.

titles.title: Operations Coordinator
titles.description: Coordinates day-to-day operational tasks, schedules, and follow-up actions to keep office or program activities running smoothly. Tracks requests, communicates with stakeholders, and helps resolve routine issues.

titles.title: Operations Support Manager
titles.description: Manages support functions that keep operations running, such as coordination, reporting, logistics, and administrative services. Improves workflows and ensures teams have the resources they need.

titles.title: Performance Metrics Analyst
titles.description: Collects and analyzes performance data to measure progress against goals and identify trends. Builds dashboards and reports that help leaders monitor results and improve operations.

titles.title: Procurement Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees procurement operations, including purchasing workflows, vendor coordination, and process compliance. Improves efficiency and ensures acquisition activities are executed consistently.

titles.title: Procurement and Budget Manager
titles.description: Manages both procurement activities and budget planning to ensure purchases align with available funding. Coordinates spending plans, acquisition actions, and financial controls.

titles.title: Project Controls Manager
titles.description: Oversees project controls such as schedules, budgets, risk tracking, and status reporting. Ensures project managers have accurate information to keep work on time and within scope.

titles.title: Project Coordinator
titles.description: Supports project managers by tracking tasks, schedules, deliverables, and meeting actions. Helps organize project documentation, communications, and status updates.

titles.title: Public Works Project Coordinator
titles.description: Coordinates public works projects by tracking schedules, permits, contractors, and project documentation. Supports communication among engineers, vendors, and stakeholders to keep projects moving.

titles.title: Quality Assurance Manager
titles.description: Leads quality assurance activities, including audits, process reviews, and corrective action tracking. Ensures products, services, or operations meet required standards.

titles.title: Recruitment Specialist
titles.description: Plans and executes recruiting activities to attract and hire qualified candidates. Posts jobs, screens applicants, coordinates interviews, and supports hiring managers.

titles.title: Regulatory Affairs Analyst
titles.description: Interprets regulations and helps ensure programs, products, or operations comply with applicable rules. Prepares compliance documentation, tracks regulatory changes, and supports responses to regulators.

titles.title: Security Program Manager
titles.description: Manages security programs, policies, and compliance activities to protect people, information, or facilities. Coordinates assessments, training, and corrective actions to reduce risk.

titles.title: Staffing Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes staffing levels, vacancies, and workforce trends to support hiring and position management decisions. Prepares reports and forecasts to help leaders meet staffing needs.

titles.title: Strategic Communications Director
titles.description: Leads communication strategy and messaging for an organization, aligning outreach with leadership priorities and mission goals. Oversees communications plans, content development, and public-facing messaging.

titles.title: Supply Chain Manager
titles.description: Manages supply chain activities such as sourcing, inventory, logistics, and vendor coordination. Works to improve availability, cost, and efficiency across the supply chain.

titles.title: Supply Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees supply operations, including inventory control, distribution, ordering, and process coordination. Ensures supplies are available when needed and operations run efficiently.

titles.title: Systems Integration Manager
titles.description: Leads efforts to connect systems, applications, or data sources so they work together effectively. Coordinates technical teams, resolves interface issues, and ensures integrated solutions meet requirements.

titles.title: Technical Program Coordinator
titles.description: Coordinates technical program activities, schedules, documentation, and stakeholder communications. Supports technical teams by tracking deliverables and helping resolve routine coordination issues.

titles.title: Technical Training Specialist
titles.description: Develops and delivers training on technical systems, tools, or procedures. Assesses training needs, creates materials, and helps users build technical proficiency.

titles.title: Technology Commercialization Specialist
titles.description: Supports the transfer of technology from development to market or operational use. Evaluates inventions, coordinates licensing or partnerships, and helps move innovations toward adoption.

titles.title: Training Operations Manager
titles.description: Manages the operational side of training delivery, including scheduling, logistics, staffing, and records. Ensures training programs run smoothly and meet organizational requirements.

titles.title: Workforce Planning Specialist
titles.description: Supports workforce planning by analyzing staffing data, position needs, and workforce trends. Helps develop forecasts, reports, and recommendations for hiring and resource planning.

titles.title: Workforce Programs Manager
titles.description: Manages workforce-related programs such as staffing, development, retention, or succession planning. Oversees program performance, policy implementation, and coordination with HR stakeholders.

titles.title: Workforce Training Coordinator
titles.description: Coordinates training schedules, enrollments, records, and communications for workforce development programs. Helps employees and managers access required training and track completion.
